title: Fix daemon shutdown and lifecycle bugs (B26, B32, B33)
description:
**B26 (HIGH): uncaughtException handler doesn't await shutdown()**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/index.ts:452-459
- shutdown() is async but not awaited in uncaughtException handler. Process exits before cleanup finishes.
- Fix: Use shutdown().then(() => process.exit(1)).catch(() => process.exit(1)) since you can't await in uncaughtException handler. Or call synchronous cleanup directly.

**B32 (MEDIUM): FileWatcher scheduleChange called after stop()**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/state/FileWatcher.ts:72-87
- No guard in scheduleChange() against processing events after stop() is called.
- Fix: Add `private stopped = false` flag. Set in stop(). Check at top of scheduleChange() and return early if stopped.

**B33 (MEDIUM): Lock file not guaranteed deleted on force shutdown**
- File: packages/moe-daemon/src/index.ts:392-405
- If releaseLock() throws during force shutdown timeout, lock file persists. Stale locks prevent new daemons.
- Fix: Use fs.unlinkSync directly as last-resort fallback if releaseLock throws. Or use fs.rmSync with force:true.
definitionOfDone: uncaughtException handler ensures cleanup before exit
Lock file always cleaned up even on crash
npm run build && npm test pass

implementationPlan.description: B26 Fix: In packages/moe-daemon/src/index.ts uncaughtException handler (~line 452-459), replace the bare `shutdown()` call with `shutdown().then(() => process.exit(1)).catch(() => process.exit(1))`. This chains the exit after async cleanup completes. Also add a safety timeout (5s) inside the handler in case shutdown() hangs: `setTimeout(() => process.exit(1), 5000).unref()`. Error handling: both .then and .catch exit with code 1 since this is a crash path.
implementationPlan.note: Replaced bare shutdown() call with shutdown().then/catch that exits with code 1. Added 5s safety timeout with unref() in case shutdown hangs.

implementationPlan.description: B32 Fix: In packages/moe-daemon/src/state/FileWatcher.ts, add `private stopped = false` field. In stop() method (~line 111-119), set `this.stopped = true` as the first line, before clearing timers. In scheduleChange() (~line 72), add early return at the top: `if (this.stopped) return;`. Also check in the debounce callback (~line 85): if stopped, don't call the onChange handler. Error handling: this is a guard pattern, no errors to handle.
implementationPlan.note: Added `stopped` flag set in stop(). scheduleChange() returns early if stopped. Debounce callback also checks stopped before calling processChange().

implementationPlan.description: B33 Fix: In packages/moe-daemon/src/index.ts force shutdown timeout (~line 392-405), after the `releaseLock()` try/catch, add a fallback that directly deletes the lock file. Import path and fs at the top (already imported). In the catch block, add: `try { const lockPath = path.join(projectPath, '.moe', 'daemon.lock'); fs.rmSync(lockPath, { force: true }); } catch {}`. The `force: true` flag means rmSync won't throw if file doesn't exist. Error handling: nested try/catch ensures we always proceed to process.exit.
implementationPlan.note: Added fs.rmSync with force:true as last-resort fallback in the catch block of releaseLock during forced shutdown. This ensures the lock file is cleaned up even if releaseLock throws.

implementationPlan.description: Add tests and verify: (1) FileWatcher test: verify that scheduleChange after stop() is a no-op. (2) Verify build: `cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build && npm test`.
implementationPlan.note: Added regression test verifying FileWatcher ignores events after stop(). 321 tests pass, build clean.
